title: Tradesviz Pricing Guide 2026: All Plans, Costs & Hidden Fees

meta_description: TradesViz pricing in 2026: Free ($0), Basic ($12.99/mo), Pro ($24.99/mo). Full plan breakdown, hidden fees, and who should upgrade.
excerpt: Complete TradesViz pricing breakdown for 2026 — free tier limits, Basic vs. Pro costs, and hidden fees options traders need to know.
content:
TradesViz Pricing Overview 2026: What You Actually Pay

TradesViz stands out in the trading journal space for one reason most platforms won't advertise loudly: its free tier is genuinely functional. You get real analytics, not a crippled demo. But before you assume free is all you need — or that the paid plans are worth skipping — this guide breaks down every plan, what's included, what's not, and where the hidden friction points are.

TradesViz earns a 4.2/5 rating on TradingToolsHub, driven by its depth of data visualization and its unusually generous free offering. The platform supports stocks, options, futures, forex, and crypto — making it one of the few journals where multi-asset traders don't get penalized for trading across markets.

Let's get into the numbers.

TradesViz Plan Breakdown 2026: Free, Basic, and Pro

TradesViz operates on a three-tier model. Here's how they stack up:

Plan Monthly Price Annual Price Trade Import Limit Best For
Free $0 $0 100 trades/month Part-time traders, trial users
Basic $12.99/mo $9.99/mo (billed annually) 1,000 trades/month Active retail traders
Pro $24.99/mo $19.99/mo (billed annually) Unlimited High-frequency traders, professionals

Annual billing saves you roughly 23% on Basic and 20% on Pro — meaningful if you're committed to the platform. Month-to-month pricing is available on all plans with no lock-in contracts.

Free Plan: What You Actually Get (And Where It Runs Out)

The TradesViz free tier isn't bait. It gives you access to over 100 performance metrics, multi-asset tracking, and the platform's signature data visualization tools — all without paying a cent. For a casual trader doing fewer than 100 trades per month, it's a legitimate long-term option.

What's included on Free:

  • Up to 100 trade imports per calendar month
  • Full access to the core analytics dashboard
  • P&L tracking across all asset classes
  • Win rate, expectancy, and risk-adjusted return metrics
  • Basic charting and trade replay
  • Manual trade entry and CSV imports from major brokers

Where Free falls short:

  • Trade volume cap: 100 trades/month sounds like a lot until you're scalping or trading options spreads with multiple legs. Each leg counts as a separate trade on some import formats.
  • No automatic broker sync: Free users must import manually or via CSV. Real-time or API-based broker connections require a paid plan.
  • Limited historical data retention: Free accounts have a rolling data window rather than indefinite historical storage.
  • No multi-account support: If you trade across multiple brokers or accounts, you'll need to upgrade to track them together.

For a deeper look at how the full feature set holds up in practice, read the TradesViz review on TradingToolsHub.

Basic vs. Pro Plan: Which Is Worth It?

The jump from Free to Basic ($12.99/month) is the most impactful upgrade on TradesViz. You get broker API connections, higher trade volume, multi-account tracking, and access to advanced filtering tools that let you slice performance by setup, time of day, market condition, and more.

Basic plan highlights:

  • Up to 1,000 trade imports per month
  • Automatic broker sync for supported brokers (including Interactive Brokers, TD Ameritrade, Tradovate, and others)
  • Multi-account portfolio view
  • Advanced tag-based trade filtering
  • Full historical data retention
  • Custom trading session grouping

Basic is the right call for most retail traders — if your monthly trade count stays under 1,000, you get everything the platform offers except unlimited volume.

Pro plan highlights:

  • Unlimited trade imports
  • Priority support queue
  • Advanced portfolio risk analytics
  • Higher API rate limits for broker connections
  • Extended data export options

Pro makes economic sense if you're trading more than 1,000 legs per month — common for options traders with multi-leg spreads, futures scalpers, or anyone running multiple strategies simultaneously. At $19.99/month on annual billing, the cost-per-insight is hard to beat in this category.

Hidden Costs and Fees to Watch For

TradesViz doesn't have a reputation for predatory pricing, but there are a few friction points worth knowing before you commit:

  • Leg counting on options: Multi-leg options strategies (spreads, iron condors, butterflies) count each leg as a separate trade import. A single iron condor = 4 trades against your monthly limit. If you're an active options trader on Free or Basic, your effective trade budget shrinks fast.
  • Currency conversion fees: If you're trading non-USD instruments and importing in a foreign currency, some import formats require manual currency mapping. No extra charge, but it adds friction.
  • Annual plan refunds: TradesViz offers annual billing at a discount, but their refund policy for unused months is limited. Review their terms before committing to a full year if you're uncertain.
  • Broker connection availability: Not every broker supports the automated API sync. If your broker isn't on the supported list, you'll be on CSV imports regardless of your plan tier.

None of these are deal-breakers — they're just worth factoring into your decision, especially if you're an options trader counting on the free tier to stay free.

How TradesViz Compares to Alternatives

TradesViz's pricing is competitive, but it's not automatically the right choice for every trader. The value depends heavily on how you use a trading journal.

Versus Edgewonk: Edgewonk operates on a one-time license fee model (around $169) rather than monthly subscriptions. If you plan to use a journal for years, Edgewonk may be cheaper long-term. TradesViz wins on data depth and multi-asset support. See the full Edgewonk vs. TradesViz comparison for a side-by-side breakdown.

Versus Journalytix: Journalytix skews toward AI-powered coaching and behavioral pattern detection. TradesViz wins on raw metrics and visualization breadth; Journalytix wins if you want automated trade feedback and pattern identification. Compare them directly in the Journalytix vs. TradesViz comparison.

Versus Chartlog: Chartlog is a simpler, more visually focused journal with a lower learning curve. TradesViz has significantly more analytical depth but can feel overwhelming to newer traders. If you're deciding between the two, the Chartlog vs. TradesViz comparison walks through the key tradeoffs.

The short version: TradesViz wins on metric depth and free-tier value. It loses on interface simplicity, AI-powered insights, and — depending on your time horizon — total cost versus one-time license models.

Who Should Pay for TradesViz in 2026?

TradesViz's pricing structure rewards a specific type of trader. Here's an honest breakdown of who should pay, who should stay free, and who should look elsewhere:

Stay on Free if:

  • You trade fewer than 100 individual legs per month
  • You're primarily testing the platform before committing
  • You trade a single asset class with a single broker

Upgrade to Basic if:

  • You need automatic broker sync to reduce manual logging friction
  • You trade across multiple accounts or brokers
  • You're serious about tracking setup performance over time (you need the historical retention)
  • You trade 100–1,000 legs per month

Go Pro if:

  • You're an active options trader with multi-leg positions
  • You're a futures scalper with high daily trade volume
  • You need maximum data export flexibility for external analysis
  • You're running multiple strategies and need the advanced portfolio risk view

Consider an alternative if:

  • You want AI-powered pattern recognition and coaching — TradesViz doesn't offer this
  • You find 100+ metrics overwhelming rather than useful — a simpler tool may serve you better
  • You're a long-term investor rather than an active trader — the platform is optimized for active trading workflows

Final Verdict: Is TradesViz Worth the Cost?

TradesViz delivers strong value at every price point — the free tier is genuinely usable, Basic at $9.99/month (annual) is one of the better deals in the trading journal market, and Pro at $19.99/month is reasonable for high-volume traders who'll actually use the unlimited import capacity.

The platform earns its 4.2/5 rating by delivering over 100 actionable metrics, excellent charting, and multi-asset support without the usual paywalled-analytics tricks. The main weaknesses — a cluttered interface and no AI analysis — are real limitations, but they don't undermine the core value proposition for data-driven traders who want depth over simplicity.

If you're a numbers-oriented trader who wants to see exactly what your setups, time-of-day patterns, and risk decisions are actually doing to your P&L, TradesViz earns a strong recommendation. Start on Free, hit the limits, then upgrade to Basic. Most traders won't need Pro.
